metadata.project: Structure-Aware Conditional VAE for Multi-Objective Therapeutic Peptide Design
feedback_addressed: Added detailed discussion of PepMorph (CVAE with masking) and PLUM (disentangled VAE with Zseq/Zfunc/Zlength)
Updated baseline R² to 0.65 from Liu et al. 2025 DMPNN benchmark
Revised novelty claims to focus on cross-attention fusion vs late fusion in MMCD/Multi-Peptide
Added comprehensive training stabilization experiments for disentangled VAEs
Included cross-attention vs late fusion ablation study
Cited MMCD (contrastive diffusion) and Multi-Peptide (multimodal prediction) as related work

primary: Permeability prediction R² > 0.70 on held-out test (vs. R² ≈ 0.65 for DMPNN baseline per Liu et al. 2025)
Property-conditioned generation achieves MAE < 0.5 log Papp units from target
>30% of generated peptides pass all three PK filters (permeable, stable, soluble) vs <15% for baselines
secondary: >80% of generated sequences achieve pLDDT > 70 in structure validation
Cross-attention fusion shows >5% improvement over late fusion ablation (replicating MMCD/Multi-Peptide approach)
Posterior collapse rate <20% using stabilization techniques vs >50% for vanilla disentangled VAE
Structure vs. sequence ablation shows >5% improvement in property prediction (R² improvement of at least 0.035)
failure_criteria: Structure vs. sequence ablation shows <3% improvement (challenges core hypothesis)
Disentangled latents collapse (>50% inactive dimensions) or fail independent control
Cross-attention provides no benefit over late fusion (<2% difference)
Generated sequences fail structural validation (<50% designable)

experiments.title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
experiments.description: Create isolated virtual environment and install all required packages for peptide modeling, structure prediction, and deep learning.

steps.step1: Create Python 3.10 virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1+ with CUDA: pip install torch==2.1.2 torchvision==0.16.2 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118
steps.step3: Install ESM-2 for sequence encoding: pip install fair-esm==2.0.0
steps.step4: Install RDKit for molecular processing: pip install rdkit==2023.9.1
steps.step5: Install PyTorch Geometric for GNN: pip install torch_geometric==2.4.0 torch-scatter torch-sparse -f https://data.pyg.org/whl/torch-2.1.0+cu118.html
steps.step6: Install structure prediction and biology tools: pip install biopython==1.81 biotite==0.37.0
steps.step7: Install evaluation utilities: pip install scikit-learn==1.3.2 scipy==1.11.4 pandas==2.1.4 numpy==1.24.3 matplotlib==3.8.2 seaborn==0.13.0
steps.step8: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(f"CUDA: {torch.cuda.is_available()}, GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)}")'

experiments.title: Download and Preprocess CycPeptMPDB Dataset
experiments.description: Download the primary cyclic peptide permeability dataset from Li et al. 2023 (JCIM) and prepare train/validation/test splits with structure generation.

steps.step1: Download CycPeptMPDB from http://cycpeptmpdb.com/download/ - get CycPeptMPDB_Peptide.csv containing 7,334 cyclic peptides
steps.step2: Filter dataset: remove sequences with >50 amino acids, remove sequences with non-standard amino acids (keep: standard 20 + D-amino acids + N-methylated)
steps.step3: Create stratified split: 70% train (~5133 peptides), 15% validation (~1100), 15% test (~1100) stratified by permeability bins (5 quantiles)
steps.step4: Generate 3D conformations using RDKit ETKDGv3 with MMFF94 optimization for all peptides; save as .sdf files in data/structures/
steps.step5: Compute molecular graphs: nodes=atoms (with features=[atomic_num, degree, formal_charge, hybridization]), edges=bonds (bond_type, conjugated, ring_status); save as PyG Data objects
steps.step6: Extract ESM-2 embeddings: use esm2_t33_650M_UR50D model (frozen), extract residue-level representations (last hidden layer, 1280-dim per residue)
steps.step7: Normalize permeability values (log Papp): compute mean/std on training set ONLY, apply normalization to all splits
steps.step8: Generate data statistics: sequence length distribution, permeability histogram, amino acid frequency, cyclic topology distribution
steps.step9: Save processed datasets: data/processed/{train,val,test}.pt with keys=['sequences', 'graphs', 'embeddings', 'labels', 'structures']

experiments.title: Prepare Auxiliary Datasets for Multi-Task Learning
experiments.description: Download and process solubility (PROSO-II) and stability datasets to enable multi-task property prediction following PLUM multi-objective approach.

steps.step1: Download PROSO-II solubility dataset (~4,000 peptide sequences with binary solubility labels)
steps.step2: Filter PROSO-II for peptides <50 amino acids, prioritize cyclic peptides where applicable
steps.step3: Curate stability dataset from literature: collect protease resistance data for ~1,000 cyclic peptides from PubMed abstracts
steps.step4: Unify data format: ensure all datasets have (sequence, graph, property_dict) structure with consistent keys
steps.step5: Create multi-task training indices: identify peptides with multiple property labels
steps.step6: Save auxiliary datasets: data/processed/prosoii.pt, data/processed/stability.pt with same structure as main dataset

experiments.title: Train Simple Baseline: Sequence-Only VAE
experiments.description: Simple baseline using LSTM-based VAE with only sequence information. Tests whether multimodal structure information is actually necessary.

steps.step1: Implement SeqVAE: Encoder=LSTM(hidden=256, layers=2, bidirectional=True), Latent dim=256, Decoder=LSTM with attention mechanism
steps.step2: VAE loss: L_recon (cross-entropy) + β*L_KL with β=1.0, property prediction head=MLP(256→128→1 for permeability)
steps.step3: Train for 100 epochs with Adam (lr=1e-3), batch_size=128, early stopping (patience=10) on validation ELBO
steps.step4: Run 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456): save best model checkpoint for each seed
steps.step5: Evaluate on test set: report R², RMSE, MAE, Pearson r for permeability prediction
steps.step6: Generate 1000 peptides from prior distribution: compute validity (correct peptide syntax), novelty (not in training set), diversity (average pairwise edit distance)

experiments.title: Train Strong Baseline: DMPNN Property Predictor
experiments.description: Reimplement DMPNN (Directed Message Passing Neural Network) from Liu et al. 2025 benchmark which achieves R² ≈ 0.65. This is the strongest published baseline for cyclic peptide permeability prediction.

steps.step1: Implement DMPNN architecture following Liu et al. 2025: 5 message passing layers, hidden_dim=300, edge features=bond types (single, double, aromatic, etc.)
steps.step2: Global pooling: concatenate mean and max pooling (600-dim) followed by MLP(600→300→1) for permeability regression
steps.step3: Train for 100 epochs with Adam (lr=1e-3), batch_size=64, MSE loss on permeability
steps.step5: Evaluate on test set: report R², RMSE, MAE, Pearson r. Target: reproduce ~R²=0.65 from Liu et al. 2025

experiments.comparison_paper: Liu et al. 2025, Journal of Cheminformatics

experiments.title: Train StruCVAE-Pep Full Model
experiments.description: Train complete StruCVAE-Pep with multimodal encoder, cross-attention fusion, disentangled latent space, and training stabilization techniques. Differs from PepMorph (sequence-only CVAE) and PLUM (sequence-only disentangled VAE) by adding explicit 3D structure via DMPNN and cross-attention fusion.

steps.step1: Initialize multimodal encoder: ESM-2_t33_650M_UR50D (frozen, extract 1280-dim per residue) + DMPNN structure encoder (5 layers, 256 hidden)
steps.step2: Implement cross-attention fusion (8 heads): structure features attend to sequence features AND vice versa, enabling early multimodal interaction
steps.step3: Define disentangled latents: z_structure (128-dim, captures conformation), z_property (64-dim, captures permeability/stability), z_sequence (64-dim, captures residue patterns)
steps.step4: Build conditional decoder: LSTM (256 hidden) with property conditioning via FiLM (Feature-wise Linear Modulation) layers
steps.step5: Set up stabilized training: composite loss L_total = L_recon + β(t)*L_KL + 0.5*L_property + 0.1*L_structure_contrastive with cyclical KL annealing
steps.step6: Apply training stabilization: (a) Cyclical annealing: β cycles from 0→1 over 5 cycles, (b) Free bits: min KL=0.5 per dimension, (c) Aggressive encoder: 2 encoder updates per decoder when KL < threshold
steps.step7: Train for 150 epochs with AdamW (lr=5e-4, weight_decay=1e-5), batch_size=64, gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0)
steps.step8: Use learning rate scheduler: CosineAnnealing with warmup (10 epochs)
steps.step9: Run 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456), save best model based on validation ELBO + property MSE
steps.step10: Log training metrics every epoch: losses, R² on validation, latent statistics (active dimensions, KL per factor)

experiments.title: Ablation: Without Structure Encoder (vs PepMorph/PLUM baseline)
experiments.description: Remove DMPNN structure encoder to isolate contribution of 3D structure information. Tests whether our multimodal approach outperforms sequence-only methods like PepMorph (CVAE) and PLUM (disentangled VAE).

steps.step1: Modify architecture: Remove structure encoder and cross-attention fusion, use only ESM-2 sequence embeddings
steps.step2: Keep all other components identical: same disentangled latent dimensions, decoder architecture, loss weights
steps.step3: Train for 150 epochs with same hyperparameters as full model
steps.step5: Compare test R² against full model: expect >5% drop if structure information is beneficial
steps.step6: Compare against published PepMorph/PLUM results to contextualize performance

experiments.title: Ablation: Cross-Attention vs Late Fusion
experiments.description: Compare cross-attention fusion (our approach) against late fusion used in MMCD (contrastive alignment) and Multi-Peptide (concatenation). Tests whether early interaction between modalities improves over late fusion.

steps.step1: Implement late fusion variant: Process sequence and structure through separate encoders (no cross-attention), concatenate final representations
steps.step2: Keep latent dimensions and total parameter count comparable to full model
steps.step3: Train for 150 epochs with same hyperparameters as full model
steps.step5: Compare R², generation quality against cross-attention model
steps.step6: Test hypothesis: cross-attention shows >5% improvement over late fusion by enabling dynamic modality interaction

experiments.title: Ablation: Without Disentanglement
experiments.description: Use single latent space instead of factorized structure/property/sequence latents. Tests whether explicit disentanglement (following PLUM's approach but with structure) improves controllability.

steps.step1: Modify architecture: Replace 3 separate latents (128+64+64=256 total) with single 256-dim latent
steps.step3: Train for 150 epochs with same hyperparameters
steps.step5: Evaluate property control: measure if conditioning on target permeability values still works effectively
steps.step6: Compare reconstruction quality vs disentangled version

experiments.title: Training Stabilization: Posterior Collapse Analysis
experiments.description: Systematically study posterior collapse in disentangled VAEs and validate that stabilization techniques prevent it. Compare against vanilla disentangled VAE without stabilization.

steps.step1: Train vanilla disentangled VAE: No cyclical annealing (β=1.0 fixed), no free bits, no aggressive encoder
steps.step2: Train with cyclical annealing only: β cycles 0→1 over 5 cycles
steps.step3: Train with free bits only: min KL=0.5 per dimension
steps.step4: Train with aggressive encoder only: 2 encoder updates per decoder update
steps.step5: Train full stabilization: All techniques combined (as in main model)
steps.step6: For each variant, run single seed (42) for 100 epochs
steps.step7: Measure posterior collapse: % of latent dimensions with KL < 0.1 (inactive)
steps.step8: Compare: reconstruction loss, KL divergence per factor, property prediction R²
steps.step9: Target: Full stabilization achieves <20% collapsed dimensions vs >50% for vanilla

stabilization_techniques.cyclical_annealing: Fu et al. 2019 - gradually increase β from 0 to 1 over multiple cycles
stabilization_techniques.free_bits: Kingma et al. 2016 - minimum KL threshold per dimension prevents collapse
stabilization_techniques.aggressive_encoder: He et al. 2019 - multiple encoder updates per decoder when encoder lags

experiments.title: Ablation: Beta (KL Weight) Sensitivity
experiments.description: Study trade-off between reconstruction quality and latent space regularization by varying β parameter.

steps.step1: Train full model with β=0.01 (very weak regularization, AE-like behavior)
steps.step2: Train full model with β=0.1 (weak regularization)
steps.step3: Train full model with β=1.0 (standard VAE)
steps.step4: Train full model with β=5.0 (strong regularization, β-VAE regime)
steps.step5: For each β, run single seed (42) for 100 epochs
steps.step6: Compare: reconstruction accuracy (perplexity), latent space smoothness (interpolation quality), property prediction performance
steps.step7: Measure disentanglement metrics: mutual information between latent factors

experiments.title: Property Prediction Benchmarking
experiments.description: Comprehensive evaluation of all models on permeability prediction with statistical significance testing against DMPNN baseline.

steps.step1: Load all trained models: SeqVAE, DMPNN, StruCVAE-Pep, ablations (3 seeds each)
steps.step2: Compute predictions on test set for all models
steps.step3: Calculate metrics for each model/seed: R², Pearson r, Spearman ρ, RMSE, MAE
steps.step4: Report mean ± std across 3 seeds for each model
steps.step5: Perform statistical testing: paired t-test between StruCVAE-Pep and each baseline
steps.step6: Compute 95% confidence intervals using bootstrap resampling (n=1000)
steps.step7: Create comparison table: Model | R² (mean±std) | RMSE | MAE | Pearson r | p-value vs DMPNN

experiments.title: Conditional Generation Evaluation
experiments.description: Test whether disentangled property latent enables controllable generation with specific permeability targets.

steps.step1: Define target permeability values: [-6.5, -6.0, -5.5, -5.0, -4.5, -4.0] log Papp (covering range from impermeable to highly permeable)
steps.step2: For each target value, generate 500 peptides using property-conditioned sampling: z_p = μ_p(y_target) + σ_p(y_target) · ε
steps.step3a: - Validity: % chemically valid (correct peptide bonds, proper cyclization)
steps.step3b: - Property MAE: mean absolute error between target and predicted permeability
steps.step3c: - Property variance: std of predicted permeabilities (lower = better control)
steps.step4: Compute aggregate: average MAE across targets, % within 0.5 log units of target, linear correlation between target and achieved
steps.step5: Compare against unconditional generation: sample from prior without property conditioning
steps.step6: Success criterion: MAE < 0.5 log units, strong correlation (r > 0.8) between target and achieved

experiments.title: Multi-Objective Generation Quality
experiments.description: Generate peptides and evaluate on multiple PK properties simultaneously. Test if joint optimization produces more 'drug-like' peptides than sequential filtering.

steps.step1: Generate 2000 peptides from full model using diverse sampling (vary z_structure and z_sequence while targeting high z_property)
steps.step2: Evaluate each generated peptide on 3 PK properties:
steps.step2a: - Permeability: predicted log Papp using trained model
steps.step2b: - Solubility: binary classifier trained on PROSO-II dataset
steps.step2c: - Stability: binary classifier trained on curated stability dataset
steps.step3: Define 'drug-like' criteria (based on literature): permeable (log Papp > -5.5), soluble (probability > 0.7), stable (probability > 0.7)
steps.step4: Compute % of generated peptides passing all three filters (target: >30%)
steps.step5: Compare against baseline SeqVAE: generate 2000 peptides and apply same filters
steps.step6: Compare against sequential pipeline: generate from SeqVAE, then filter with DMPNN predictor
steps.step7: Compute diversity metrics: pairwise sequence edit distance (Levenshtein), physicochemical property variance (hydrophobicity, charge, size)
steps.step8: Compute novelty: % of generated peptides not in training set (similarity < 0.8)

experiments.title: Latent Space Analysis and Disentanglement Verification
experiments.description: Analyze learned latent spaces to verify disentanglement following PLUM's methodology but extended to 3D structure.

steps.step1: Encode all test peptides into latent space using trained StruCVAE-Pep
steps.step2: Compute correlation between z_structure dimensions and structural properties:
steps.step3: Compute correlation between z_property dimensions and PK properties: permeability, solubility, stability
steps.step4a: - I(z_structure; z_property) should be low (structure independent of property)
steps.step4b: - I(z_structure; z_sequence) should be low (structure independent of sequence patterns)
steps.step4c: - I(z_property; z_sequence) should be low (property independent of sequence patterns)
steps.step5: Perform linear interpolation in latent space: traverse z_property dimension while keeping z_structure, z_sequence fixed; verify smooth permeability transition
steps.step6: Visualize latent space: t-SNE/UMAP projection colored by permeability bins, structure clusters, and sequence motifs

experiments.title: Structure Validation with ESMFold
experiments.description: Validate that generated sequences adopt designable 3D structures. Use ESMFold as lightweight alternative to AlphaFold2.

steps.step1: Select top 100 generated peptides by predicted permeability (high, medium, low bins)
steps.step2: Run ESMFold structure prediction for each selected peptide (install via pip install esm fold if needed, or use API)
steps.step3: Compute pLDDT (predicted Local Distance Difference Test) scores for each prediction
steps.step4: Compute TM-scores between predicted structures and reference cyclic peptide structures from PDB
steps.step5: Report statistics: % with pLDDT > 70 (designable threshold), % with pLDDT > 90 (high confidence)
steps.step6: Analyze relationship between predicted permeability and structural confidence
steps.step7: Visualize example structures: show high-confidence (pLDDT > 90) and low-confidence (pLDDT < 50) examples

experiments.title: Generate Paper Figures
experiments.description: Create publication-quality figures comparing against baselines and related work (PepMorph, PLUM, MMCD approaches).

steps.step1: Figure 1: Model architecture diagram showing cross-attention fusion between ESM-2 and DMPNN, disentangled latents (z_structure, z_property, z_sequence)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Property prediction comparison - bar chart of R² for all models with error bars (3 seeds), highlight DMPNN baseline (R²≈0.65) from Liu et al. 2025
steps.step3: Figure 3: Conditional generation results - scatter plot of target vs achieved permeability, with R² and MAE annotated
steps.figure3_caption: Demonstrates disentangled property latent enables controllable generation unlike PepMorph (descriptor-only conditioning)
steps.step4: Figure 4: Ablation study results - performance drop when removing each component (structure, disentanglement, cross-attention)
steps.figure4_caption: Cross-attention vs Late Fusion comparison highlighting advantage over MMCD/Multi-Peptide approaches
steps.step5: Figure 5: Training stabilization comparison - KL divergence per factor over training for vanilla vs stabilized VAE
steps.figure5_caption: Shows <20% posterior collapse rate with stabilization vs >50% without
steps.step6: Figure 6: Latent space visualization - t-SNE with permeability coloring and interpolation trajectory
steps.step7: Figure 7: Generated peptide examples - 2D structures of high-scoring drug-like designs
steps.step8: Table 1: Main results with mean±std across 3 seeds, 95% CI, and p-values vs baselines
steps.step9: Table 2: Comparison with published methods (DMPNN from Liu et al. 2025, PepMorph, PLUM concepts)

experiments.title: Statistical Significance and Error Analysis
experiments.description: Rigorous statistical testing and comprehensive error analysis to support claims.

steps.step1: Compute 95% confidence intervals for all metrics using bootstrap resampling (n=1000 samples)
steps.step2: Perform paired t-tests between StruCVAE-Pep and each baseline (DMPNN, SeqVAE)
steps.step3: Apply Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons if testing against >2 baselines
steps.step4: Analyze failure cases: identify peptide subgroups where model performs poorly (e.g., by length, by amino acid composition)
steps.step5: Correlation analysis: relationship between sequence length and prediction error, between structural complexity and error
steps.step6: Generate final summary report: mean ± std ± 95% CI for all key metrics with significance markers

parallel_groups.description: Environment and data setup (sequential, must complete first)
experiments: Prepare Auxiliary Datasets for Multi-Task Learning

parallel_groups.description: Baseline training (parallel on single GPU, sequential execution)
experiments: Train Strong Baseline: DMPNN Property Predictor

parallel_groups.description: Main model and key ablations (primary experiments)
experiments: Ablation: Without Structure Encoder (vs PepMorph/PLUM baseline)

parallel_groups.description: Secondary ablations (can run after group 3 completes)
Evaluation and analysis (after all training completes)
experiments: Latent Space Analysis and Disentanglement Verification

execution_plan.total_estimated_time: ~7.5 hours with careful sequential execution on 1 GPU
execution_plan.critical_path: Group 1 → Group 2/3 (partially parallel) → Group 4/5 → Group 6 → Group 7

PepMorph.approach: Transformer-based CVAE with masking mechanism for morphology-aware peptide generation
PepMorph.key_features: ESM-2 alphabet, masking for partial conditioning, aggregation propensity prediction
PepMorph.our_difference: PepMorph is sequence-only with descriptor conditioning; StruCVAE-Pep adds explicit 3D structure encoder (DMPNN) with cross-attention fusion and targets PK properties (permeability, stability) rather than aggregation morphology

PLUM.approach: Disentangled VAE with Zseq (sequence), Zfunc (function), Zlength latents for antimicrobial peptides
PLUM.key_features: Adversarial predictors with gradient reversal, LSTM encoder/decoder, 88.5% AMP yield
PLUM.our_difference: PLUM uses sequence-only inputs (LSTM-based); StruCVAE-Pep adds explicit 3D structure encoding via DMPNN and cross-attention fusion; targets therapeutic PK properties vs antimicrobial activity

MMCD.approach: Multi-modal contrastive diffusion for peptide sequence-structure co-generation
MMCD.key_features: Late fusion via inter-contrastive (sequence-structure alignment) and intra-contrastive (therapeutic vs non-therapeutic) learning
MMCD.our_difference: MMCD uses diffusion with late fusion; StruCVAE-Pep uses VAE with early cross-attention fusion and explicit disentanglement of structure vs property factors; enables direct conditioning on continuous values

Multi_Peptide.approach: PeptideBERT + GNN with contrastive learning for peptide property prediction
Multi_Peptide.key_features: AlphaFold structures, late fusion via embedding concatenation, prediction-only task
Multi_Peptide.our_difference: Multi-Peptide is a prediction model with late fusion; StruCVAE-Pep is a generative model with early cross-attention fusion and end-to-end trainable architecture

SaProt.contribution: Structure-aware protein language model using Foldseek 3Di tokens
SaProt.our_extension: Apply structure-aware representations to conditional peptide generation with cross-modal fusion

Liu_et_al_2025.paper: Liu et al. 2025, Journal of Cheminformatics
Liu_et_al_2025.finding: DMPNN achieves R² ≈ 0.65 on random split of CycPeptMPDB, outperforming other methods in comprehensive benchmark of 13 ML models
Liu_et_al_2025.our_target: Exceed DMPNN with R² > 0.70 using multimodal cross-attention approach

training_stabilization_details.problem: Disentangled VAEs suffer from posterior collapse where latent dimensions become inactive (KL approaches 0)
training_stabilization_details.why_it_matters: Collapse prevents disentangled representation learning, making property control impossible

techniques.name: Cyclical KL Annealing
techniques.description: Gradually increase β from 0 to 1 over multiple cycles (5 cycles, each with 30 epochs warmup)
techniques.purpose: Allow model to learn good reconstructions before full KL regularization kicks in

techniques.name: Free Bits
techniques.description: Set minimum KL threshold of 0.5 nats per latent dimension
techniques.purpose: Prevent any single dimension from collapsing entirely

techniques.name: Aggressive Encoder Training
techniques.description: Perform 2 encoder updates per decoder update when KL < 1.0 (encoder is 'lagging')
techniques.purpose: Ensure encoder keeps up with decoder to prevent posterior mismatch

techniques.name: Decoder Weakening
techniques.description: Randomly drop 20% of conditioning tokens during decoding
techniques.purpose: Force decoder to rely on latent variables rather than autoregressive dependencies

techniques.name: Factor-specific β
techniques.description: Use β=2.0 for z_property (encourage disentanglement), β=0.5 for z_structure/z_sequence (preserve reconstruction)
techniques.purpose: Balance disentanglement vs reconstruction quality per factor

training_stabilization_details.measurement: Track % of latent dimensions with KL < 0.1 (inactive) per factor over training
training_stabilization_details.target: <20% collapsed dimensions with full stabilization vs >50% for vanilla VAE
